title: Omit<T, K> preserves optionality of remaining props
description: Omit preserves the optionality of remaining properties — resolves to {b?: number; c: boolean}.
notes: `c` stays required and `b` stays optional after the omit, so a value missing `c` FAILS while a value missing `b` passes.
pureType: createValidate<Omit<{a: string; b?: number; c: boolean}, 'a'>>()
schema: createValidate(RT.omit(RT.object({a: RT.string(), b: RT.optional(RT.number()), c: RT.boolean()}), ['a']))
generated: function YOw_lbV5zhx(v){return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && (v.b === undefined || Number.isFinite(v.b)) && typeof v.c === 'boolean')}
